key_insight: Nonconvex stochastic optimization can be reduced in a black-box way to ordinary static-regret online convex optimization by learning a preconditioner for a gradient tracker.
engineering_relevance: This gives a modular framework for building adaptive optimizers, clarifying how methods like AdaGrad and Shampoo can be viewed through online preconditioner selection for practical nonconvex training.
method: Predict a stochastic gradient tracker for the nonconvex objective.
Use an online convex learner to choose a preconditioner that transforms the tracker into the update direction.
Require only static regret against one fixed comparator, not stronger online regret notions.
evidence_signal: For β-smooth objectives with bounded range and bounded-variance stochastic gradients, the method achieves a gradient-norm bound that matches the classical O(1/√T) rate whenever the OCO oracle has O(√T) static regret; for Lipschitz non-smooth nonconvex objectives it achieves the optimal O(T^-2/7) Goldstein-stationary rate.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the online regret oracle and on access to an unbiased stochastic-gradient oracle, so practical gains may vary with the chosen preconditioner learner.
paper_title: Optimizing the Preconditioner: A Black-box Online-to-Nonconvex Conversion with Static Regret Minimization Oracles
